mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 4%, the increased cost of capital and tighter financial conditions compress corporate profit margins and equity valuation multiples, leading to negative forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The FOMC has raised the Fed Funds rate to 4.16% in a steady tightening cycle since 2004.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) in periods following the FEDFUNDS rate crossing 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market, which increases wage pressures. This compresses corporate profit margins, leading to lower future earnings expectations and negative equity returns as the business cycle peaks.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate is at a cyclical low of 4.9%.
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE is below 5% are not followed by below-average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of extremely high housing starts and a rising federal funds rate creates an unsustainable dynamic for the housing market. Rising rates will choke off housing demand, leading to a sharp contraction in construction and related consumption, negatively impacting the broader market.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2000 and FEDFUNDS is in a rising trend
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are near all-time highs while the Fed is tightening aggressively.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not experience a significant drawdown (>10%) within 12 months following periods where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects strong business activity and output. This translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity returns over the near term.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly INDPRO growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation and the policy rate are rising concurrently, it signals a restrictive monetary regime aimed at slowing the economy. This dual pressure of rising input costs (inflation) and rising discount rates (Fed policy) is negative for equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0 and 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been hiking since mid-2004 to combat rising inflation pressures.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not statistically lower during periods of concurrent increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a primary real-time indicator of economic strength, signaling robust corporate activity and consumer income growth, which drives positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of monthly PAYEMS changes for 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.03 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates an unexpected contraction in consumer spending, a primary driver of the US economy. This negative surprise leads to downward revisions of earnings estimates and negative stock returns.
failure_condition: Instances where monthly retail sales fall by more than 1% are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days in a statistically significant majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is already tight (low unemployment), an acceleration in inflation forces the central bank into a more hawkish stance than the market anticipates. This 'overheating' signal leads to a repricing of rate expectations and a drop in equity prices.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 4.9% and inflation has been a persistent concern for the FOMC.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not underperform its long-term average in the 20 trading days following the trigger condition being met.
